summary.intent: Investigate and fix GitHub issue #766 in the moltis repo — a missing HTTP endpoint for Slack slash commands that was documented in the manifest but never implemented.
summary.outcome: Fully implemented the missing `POST /api/channels/slack/{account_id}/commands` HTTP route across three files, including form-body parsing, plugin delegation, gateway route registration, tests, and a clean commit. All 367 tests pass.

repo: The Slack integration has three HTTP routes (events, interactions, commands) but only two were registered in gateway.rs — commands was handled via Socket Mode websocket path only.
Slash commands arrive as application/x-www-form-urlencoded, not JSON, unlike events and interactions which are JSON payloads.
The dedup middleware skips deduplication when idempotency_key is None — safe for slash commands since they don't carry an event_id and are not retried by Slack like Events API payloads.
SlackAccountConfig derives Default, allowing test setup with field overrides rather than fully specifying all fields.
The manifest snippet in crates/slack/src/commands.rs:60 already referenced the correct URL — the endpoint just didn't exist.
Socket Mode handler in socket.rs:262 uses '{command} {text}'.trim() pattern for combining command fields — HTTP handler should match this.

code.finding: Third Slack route (commands) was missing here; events and interactions routes existed but commands was never registered.
New handle_verified_command_webhook function added here, with URL-encoded form body parsing and dispatch_command delegation.
New ingest_verified_command_webhook method added following the same delegation pattern as ingest_verified_interaction_webhook.
command_events_callback handles slash commands via websocket — the full_command construction pattern to replicate in HTTP handler.
SlackAccountConfig has many fields but derives Default, simplifying test setup.

workflow: Used worktrees under /Users/penso/.superset/worktrees/moltis/ for isolated development.
Compiled each affected crate independently before running the full test suite to catch errors early.
Clippy caught a real issue after initial tests passed — always run clippy before committing.
No Dolt remote was configured in the worktree, so pull from main was a no-op — acceptable for local worktree workflow.
Used beads issue tracking: created issue, claimed it, then closed it after commit.

friction: Initial test used fully-qualified type names (crate::config::SlackAccountConfig) but `use super::*` already imported them — caused a compile error that required an edit pass.
No existing extract_form_field helper existed in webhook.rs — had to add a new generic form field extractor.
open_items: Slack slash commands expect a response within 3 seconds; if dispatch_command is slow, the HTTP handler may time out. No async response / delayed response mechanism was implemented.

The HTTP command handler response format returns plain text — could be enhanced to return structured JSON with response_type: ephemeral to control visibility, matching what Socket Mode SlackCommandEventResponse would produce.
No integration-level test covering the full gateway route + middleware pipeline for the new /commands endpoint — only unit tests in webhook.rs were added.
